actor_claims.quote_text: King & Spalding LLP ("King & Spalding"), legal counsel to the Company

actor_relation_claims.quote_text: The indication of interest was supported by a highly confident financing letter from Jefferies Finance LLC

bid_claims.quote_text: On April 14, 2014, five parties, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest. The bids received in the preliminary indications of interest ranged from $20.00 per share to $22.00 per share.

On May 9, 2014, Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest to BofA Merrill Lynch, increasing the number of preliminary indications of interest received by us as of such date to six. The bid submitted by Party X ranged from $21.50 to $23.00 per share.

On May 20, 2014, Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest, bringing the total number of preliminary indications of interest received by us to seven. The indication of interest submitted by Party Y ranged from $19.50 to $20.50 per share.
On February 19, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to the Company to acquire us for a per share price of $19.25.

event_claims.description: The board first discussed exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: At a June 20, 2013 meeting, our board of directors first discussed the possibility of exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.

event_claims.description: The board unanimously agreed to proceed with initial steps related to exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale.
event_claims.quote_text: Our board of directors unanimously agreed to proceed with certain initial steps related to exploring our strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.

event_claims.description: The board decided to engage in a process to consider strategic alternatives and approved BofA Merrill Lynch as financial advisor.
event_claims.quote_text: At a January 28, 2014 board meeting, our board of directors discussed our year to date performance, our anticipated performance against the fiscal year 2014 plan and various factors that could impact our future performance. Following discussions, our board of directors decided to engage in a process to consider strategic alternatives.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch contacted fifty potential buyers at the board's direction.
event_claims.quote_text: During the next several weeks, BofA Merrill Lynch, at the direction of our board of directors, contacted fifty potential buyers (comprising twenty-eight strategic buyers and twenty-two financial buyers, including financial buyers who owned businesses similar to our businesses), and we executed confidentiality agreements with a number of the potential buyers.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch distributed a first round process letter setting an April 14, 2014 deadline for preliminary indications of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: On March 27, 2014, at the direction of our board of directors, BofA Merrill Lynch distributed a first round process letter, which informed potential buyers that non-binding preliminary indications of interest would be due no later than April 14, 2014.

event_claims.description: Five parties submitted preliminary non-binding indications of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 14, 2014, five parties, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest.

event_claims.description: Party X submitted a preliminary non-binding indication of interest, increasing total preliminary indications to six.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 9, 2014, Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest to BofA Merrill Lynch, increasing the number of preliminary indications of interest received by us as of such date to six.

event_claims.description: Party X withdrew from pursuing a potential transaction before diligence access or management meetings.
event_claims.quote_text: However, on May 14, 2014, prior to receiving access to the electronic data room or meeting with the Company's management, Party X informed representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested in pursuing a potential transaction with the Company.

event_claims.description: Party Y submitted a preliminary non-binding indication of interest, bringing total preliminary indications to seven.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 20, 2014, Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest, bringing the total number of preliminary indications of interest received by us to seven.

event_claims.description: The board concluded it would consider granting New Mountain Capital a thirty day exclusivity period if New Mountain Capital raised its offer price and confirmed a go-shop provision.
event_claims.quote_text: After further discussion, our board of directors concluded that it would consider granting New Mountain Capital a thirty day exclusivity period if New Mountain Capital raised its offer price and confirmed that a "go-shop" provision would be included in any potential definitive merger agreement.

On May 22, 2014, a draft of the merger agreement was distributed to the six remaining bidders (Party X having withdrawn from the process on May 14, 2014) that had submitted preliminary indications of interest.
